description: allowed-tools declares Bash(npx agent-browser:*) but line 25 of the body explicitly prohibits npx: 'Always use agent-browser directly -- never npx agent-browser'
suggested_fix: Remove Bash(npx agent-browser:*) from allowed-tools to match the documented restriction

description: No allowed-tools field in frontmatter; all five other SKILL.md files declare Bash(agent-browser:*) and Bash(npx agent-browser:*); omission may be intentional since skill teaches TypeScript SDK patterns rather than direct CLI invocations
suggested_fix: Add allowed-tools if the agent needs Bash access during TypeScript scaffolding, or add a comment in frontmatter noting the omission is intentional

description: postinstall lifecycle hook auto-executes node scripts/postinstall.js on every npm install; script performs network download of a native binary and rewrites global bin symlinks/shims
suggested_fix: Document postinstall behavior in README security section; consider opt-in download flag for security-sensitive environments

description: symlinkSync replaces the agent-browser symlink in the npm global bin directory (system PATH location) at install time via fixUnixSymlink()
suggested_fix: Standard npm native-binary distribution pattern; acceptable if binary provenance is verified via published checksums

description: writeFileSync overwrites .cmd and .ps1 shims in the Windows global npm prefix dir outside the package directory via fixWindowsShims()
suggested_fix: Standard Windows npm shim optimization; acceptable if binary provenance is verified via published checksums

description: Downloads platform-specific native binary from GitHub Releases over HTTPS; URL constructed from package.json version field; no SHA256 checksum verification before chmodSync makes file executable
suggested_fix: Publish SHA256 checksums alongside GitHub Releases and verify digest before calling chmodSync

description: execSync invoked with shell command string 'ldd --version 2>&1 || true' for musl libc detection inside isMusl()
fp_reason: Command string is completely hardcoded with no user-controlled interpolation; standard platform detection pattern with no injection surface
rule_gap: Rule should exempt execSync calls where the entire command string is a compile-time constant with no template interpolation

description: COMMAND=$* captures all user-supplied CLI args; value is passed via Python json.dump to AWS SSM and executed as PowerShell on a remote EC2 instance; intentional developer debug tool
suggested_fix: Add a header echo warning naming the target EC2 instance before dispatching the command so the caller knows where code will run
